title: WorkItemDependency
description: Blocking dependency between two work items. Kept as a separate join table (not embedded in WorkItem) to support efficient bi-directional queries and the many-to-1 fan-in pattern where multiple work items of one type are blocked by a single work item of another type. Example: multiple SETTLEMENT_EXCEPTION items blocked by one SSI_SETUP_REQUEST. PostgreSQL indexes required: (tenantId, blockingWorkItemId) for fan-out query on blocking item completion; (tenantId, dependentWorkItemId) for display query showing what a given item is waiting for.

dependentWorkItemId.description: The work item that is blocked and waiting.

dependentWorkflowType.description: Denormalised for efficient queries without joining work_items.

blockingWorkItemId.description: The work item that must reach a terminal state before the dependent can resume.

blockingWorkflowType.description: Denormalised for efficient queries without joining work_items.

deduplicationKey.description: The computed value (e.g. counterparty LEI) that was used to match this dependent to an existing blocking work item rather than creating a new one. Stored for audit and debugging. Set by the CREATE_CHILD_WORKFLOW action when deduplicationKeyField is configured.

status.description: RESOLVED when the blocking work item reaches a terminal state and the dependent has been re-triggered via CHILD_WORKFLOW_COMPLETED system event.

resolvedAt.description: Set when status transitions to RESOLVED. Null while ACTIVE.
